title: Dryopteris gracilescens
taxonomy: Dryopteris gracilescens is a species of fern in the genus Dryopteris, which belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns that have multiple leaflets on their fronds. The class Polypodiopsida is the class to which this species belongs, and it is further classified under the division Pteridophyta. The kingdom Plantae is the broader classification for Dryopteris gracilescens, which is a group of plants that includes ferns and their allies. The taxonomy of Dryopteris gracilescens is not yet fully resolved, and further research may lead to changes in its classification.

intro: Dryopteris gracilescens, also known as Japanese shield fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of D. gracilescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, D. gracilescens is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is commonly used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
